content:
- Continued task 34 from TO BE VERIFIED marker; performed a deep skeptical verification pass with broad parallel source probes and changed the plan before switching marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Implemented runtime entrypoint surface: added `src/runtime/mod.rs` + `src/runtime/node.rs`, exported `pub mod runtime` from `src/lib.rs`, and added new production binary `src/bin/pgtuskmaster.rs` that runs `--config <path>`.
- Added startup planner/executor in runtime with strict Result-based errors: data-dir inspection (missing/empty/existing/ambiguous), DCS startup probe using `EtcdDcsStore::connect + drain_watch_events + refresh_from_etcd_watch`, startup mode selection (`InitializePrimary`, `CloneReplica`, `ResumeExisting`), and startup command execution through process-worker command builder path.
- Added runtime unit tests covering planner/data-dir matrix (including ambiguous partial dir failure) and clone/initialize/resume decision behavior.
- Extended process/config surface for replica bootstrap: added `pg_basebackup` to `BinaryPaths` + parser/default validation and all touched fixtures/examples; added `BaseBackupSpec` + `ProcessJobKind::BaseBackup` + command/timeout/active-kind wiring in process worker, and added basebackup command unit test.
- Added binary smoke coverage for the new node binary in `tests/cli_binary.rs` (`node_help_exits_success`).
- Ran formatting and all required gates successfully with flake mitigations:
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 make check` PASS
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 make test` PASS (full suite including real HA e2e)
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 make test-bdd` PASS
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 make lint` PASS (all strict clippy passes)
- Updated task file checklists/status to done with `<passing>true</passing>`, updated AGENTS.md with new learning about `BinaryPaths` field additions affecting examples under `--all-targets`, and ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`.
- Next immediate step: stage all modified files (including .ralph files), commit with required message format including evidence/challenges, push to remote, and stop.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and evaluated selection rules in order: bug-first, priority tags, and meta-task precedence.
- Confirmed all bug tasks currently show <passes>true</passes>/<passing>true</passing>; no failing bug to prioritize.
- Found active non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>; highest-priority unblocked option is task 35 (priority high), with task 36 blocked by 35.
- Wrote only this path to .ralph/current_task.txt: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/35-task-migrate-all-node-startup-tests-to-unified-entrypoint-config-only.md.
- Next cycle should execute task 35; after that, task 36 becomes the next high-priority follow-up in the same chain.

- Picked up new task 35 from .ralph/current_task.txt and confirmed task file had no TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE marker, so this turn was planning mode only.
- Ran broad parallel inventory sweeps (10+ tracks) across src/tests/examples for node startup paths, worker-run orchestration, direct initdb/pg_ctl usage, runtime entrypoint surface, and policy guards.
- Verified primary startup-bypass hotspot is src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs (manual context wiring + direct initialize_pgdata path) and collected supporting/non-target files (policy guard, harness binaries, contract/API tests).
- Wrote full Detailed Implementation Plan (Draft 1) into task 35 with scope clarification, file-by-file change plan, execution phases, skeptical risk mitigations, and explicit completion checklist.
- Appended TO BE VERIFIED at end of task file exactly per requested workflow and stopped before execution/verification phase.
- Next step on resume: read TO BE VERIFIED and do deep skeptical verification, alter at least one plan item, then switch marker to NOW EXECUTE.

- Handled incoming PO email investigation task ("U ok?") strictly as research/status, no code fixes.
- Ran startup memory read via .ralph/progress_read.sh.
- Sent frequent PO replies during the task: immediate ack, plan, subtask start/completion, mid-investigation, and final summary.
- Verified process management status via receive_mail/agent-status.sh: cedar-cobra active and running.
- Verified Ralph service status: ralph-pgtuskmaster.service active since Tue 2026-03-03 08:15:28 CET.
- Inspected live journal output and evidence/log files:
  - Confirmed logs moved recently and continuously (not dead).
  - Observed make test completed at 14:43:51 CET with 3 HA e2e timeout failures.
  - Observed immediate follow-up loop (reasoning "Increasing timeouts for stability", file update src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs, then cargo check success at 14:44:20 CET).
  - Confirmed evidence files in .ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress updated through 14:43:22 CET.
- Reported to PO what Ralph is doing, what it is waiting for, why it appears slow (long real HA e2e runs + retry loop), and precise timestamps.
- Next if asked: continue monitoring same service journal and evidence mtimes; no intervention requested right now.

- Investigated PO complaint about timeout-based HA e2e stabilization; treated this as research only, no code edits.
- Sent frequent email updates to PO per workflow: immediate ack, plan, midpoint findings, subtask completion, final synthesis start, final summary.
- Confirmed test fixture already forces fast HA loop (`loop_interval_ms: 100`) in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` and runtime workers consume that config (`src/runtime/node.rs` poll intervals).
- Confirmed 400s-ish run time can come from additive failure-path wait budgets across stress scenarios (120s switchover wait + 180s failover wait + 90s no-quorum wait + overhead), not that one failover necessarily takes 400s.
- Aggregated `.ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress/*.summary.json`: many passes are fast for switchover/failover (~22-27s), while failures are intermittent and dominated by API polling/state availability errors (connection refused/read timeout/reset) plus follow-on table/readiness timeouts.
- Noted likely contributor: cluster HA state polling is sequential across nodes (`cluster_ha_states`), and HTTP steps each permit up to 20s, which can expand a single polling cycle under instability.
- Did not create or modify code/tasks because PO request was investigative/questioning; only communicated findings.
- Next if asked: convert findings into explicit implementation task to replace timeout-bump strategy with deterministic polling/readiness fixes and stronger failure diagnostics.

- Processed PO operational request from email to read continue-ralph skill but NOT execute full loop actions.
- PO explicitly directed that timeout-strategy retries must stop because they take far too long and do not seem to help.
- Intentionally skipping task switch/commit/stage actions; will only restart ralph service (stop then start) and report status.
- Next after this append: perform service stop/start and confirm active state.

- Completed PO-requested limited continue action: read continue-ralph skill but intentionally did NOT run staging/commit/task-switch steps.
- Restart operation detail: `systemctl --user stop ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` succeeded; direct `systemctl --user start` failed because transient unit was removed (`not found`) after stop.
- Recreated service using the same `systemd-run --user --unit=ralph-pgtuskmaster --collect ... /bin/bash .ralph/ralph-worker.sh codex` pattern as ralph.sh.
- Verified service active with ActiveEnterTimestamp Tue 2026-03-03 14:57:46 CET and confirmed agent process still running.
- Preserved PO constraints: no commit, no task switch, no codebase fix work.
